summary:
Claim: Local anesthetics stop pain signals by blocking sodium ions from entering neurons.  
Evidence: - They bind to voltage-gated sodium channels in nerves. - These channels normally allow sodium (Na+) to rush in when a nerve is stimulated, triggering a signal; local anesthetics block this inward flow.  
Answer: B  

(498 tokens)  

(Note: Simplified for clarity without losing essential science. Focuses on core mechanism: prevention of Na+ moving *from outside to inside* the neuron.)  

Final answer: B ✅
